title: Multi-modal polyisoolefin compositions and processes therefor
abstract: A polyisoolefin-based polymer composition having a multimodal molecular weight distribution is described, which can be prepared by the late addition of a chain transfer agent to the polymerization process. The polymer composition has a low molecular weight fraction having a peak molecular weight (M p ) of less than about 100,000 g/mol and a high molecular weight fraction having a peak molecular weight (M p ) of greater than about 250,000 g/mol, the low molecular weight fraction present in the composition in an amount less than the high molecular weight fraction. Cured articles produced from the polyisoolefin-based polymer composition exhibit improved processability while maintaining physical properties.
claims: Claims Claims: 1 . A polyisoolefin-based polymer composition comprising a multimodal molecular weight distribution comprising a low molecular weight fraction having a peak molecular weight (M p ) of less than about 100,000 g/mol and a high molecular weight fraction having a peak molecular weight (M p ) of greater than about 250,000 g/mol, the low molecular weight fraction present in the composition in an amount less than the high molecular weight fraction. 2. The composition according to claim 1 having a bimodal molecular weight distribution. 3. The composition according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the low molecular weight fraction is present in an amount of 5-50 wt%, preferably 15-25 wt%, based on total weight of polymer. 4. The composition according to any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein the polyisoolefin- based polymer composition comprises polyisobutylene. 5. The composition according to any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein the polyisoolefin- based polymer composition comprises a copolymer of at least one isoolefin monomer, preferably isobutene, and at least one other monomer, preferably Î²-pinene or at least one multiolefin monomer. 6. The composition according to claim 5, wherein the copolymer is brominated or chlorinated. 7. The composition according to claim 5 or 6, wherein the at least one multiolefin monomer comprises isoprene. 8. The composition according to any one of claims 5 to 7, wherein the copolymer further comprises a cationically polymerizable monomer, preferably Î±-methyl styrene, p- methyl styrene, chlorostyrene or mixtures thereof. 9. The composition according to any one of claims 5 to 8, wherein the low molecular weight fraction comprises more of the at least one other monomer than the high molecular weight fraction. 10. The composition according to any one of claims 1 to 9, wherein the low molecular weight fraction is substantially branched. 1 1 . The composition according to any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein the low molecular weight fraction comprises one or more branching agents, preferably a multifunctional oligomer, a multiolefin crosslinking agent or a mixture thereof, more preferably divinylbenzene. 12. The composition according to any one of claims 1 to 1 1 , further comprising auxiliary products for polymers. 13. A process for producing a polyisoolefin-based polymer composition having a multimodal molecular weight distribution, the process comprising: polymerizing an isoolefin in a first step in the absence of a chain transfer agent; and, continuing to polymerize the isoolefin in a second step in the presence of a chain transfer agent to produce a polyisoolefin-based polymer composition comprising a multimodal molecular weight distribution comprising a low molecular weight fraction having a peak molecular weight (M p ) of less than about 100,000 g/mol and a high molecular weight fraction having a peak molecular weight (M p ) of greater than about 250,000 g/mol, the low molecular weight fraction present in the composition in an amount less than the high molecular weight fraction. 14. The process according to claim 13, wherein the polymerizing is a solution polymerization process, a slurry polymerization process, a batch process or a continuous process such as a plug flow process. 15. The process according to claim 13 or 14, wherein the first step is conducted for a time in a range of 10-45 minutes. 16. The process according to any one of claims 13 to 15, wherein the chain transfer agent is added to the process when 25% or more conversion of monomers to polymer has been achieved, and the chain transfer agent preferably comprises 2,4,4-trimethyM - pentene. 17. The process according to any one of claims 13 to 16, wherein one or more co- monomers and/or branching agents are added to the process after polymerization has been initiated. 18. The process according to any one of claims 13 to 17, wherein the isoolefin is isobutene, and the isobutene is preferably copolymerized with Î²-pinene or at least one multiolefin monomer, preferably isoprene. 19. The process according to any one of claims 13 to 18, further comprising brominating or chlorinating the polyisoolefin-based polymer. 20. A cured article comprising a polyisoolefin-based polymer composition as defined in any one of claims 1 to 12, the article preferably comprising a tire inner liner, a tire inner tube, seals, a gasket or a pharmaceutical closure.
